calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Writ Of Administrative Mandate Pursuant To Ccp 1094.5
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER RUTH RODGERS' Motion For Judgment On Writ Of Administrative Mandate Pursuant To CCP 1094.5:

Petitioner Rodgers' petition for writ of administrative mandamus is denied.

The Court employs the independent judgment test to determine whether the weight of the evidence supports respondent's findings. (See Yakov v. Board of Medical Examiners (1968) 68 Cal.2d 67, 69.) Respondent's findings bear a strong presumption of correctness and the burden rests upon petitioner to prove that the findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. (See Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 817.)

The weight of the evidence supports respondent's decision. The record indicates that petitioner has not abused illegal drugs or alcohol during her probation and she should be lauded for that. (AR 179.) Nonetheless, petitioner's present sobriety does not render respondent's concerns regarding public safety, petitioner's future compliance, or its penalty of an additional year of probation an abuse of discretion.

The Decision finds there were 26 separate instances in which petitioner failed to check in for testing with the monitoring laboratory as required and 16 separate instances in which petitioner failed to test as ordered. (AR 158-159, 161, 352-353.) The ALJ who heard her testimony found that petitioner's explanations for these numerous probation violations are "not compelling." (AR 359.) As the ALJ concluded, "[Petitioner's] lack of diligence and negligence go to the very conditions of probation which were imposed because she was found to be a physician with a substance use disorder. Her lack of compliance has deprived the Board of the assurance it needs in order to be sure that [petitioner] can practice safely." (Id.) Extending petitioner's probation for one year is a reasonable and measured response to petitioner's derelictions to ensure her sobriety and public safety. Business & Professions Code section 2229, subdivisions (b) & (c) expressly allows for discipline to further rehabilitation.

The discipline for misconduct imposed by an administrative agency will not be disturbed in a mandamus proceeding unless a manifest abuse of discretion is demonstrated. (See Landau v. Medical Board (1998) 81 Cal.App.4th 191, 217-218 [finding no manifest abuse of discretion in revocation of physician's license, despite passage of nearly 10 years since last incident charged, where "reasonable minds could differ over the appropriateness of the penalty imposed"]; see also Oduyale v. California State Bd. of Pharmacy (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 101, 117-122.) Petitioner fails to show such an abuse.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion For Stay And Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Carr, Deputy Attorney General, 415-510-3380 of the Office of the Attorney General for Repondent Medical Board of California.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER RUTH RODGERS Motion For Preliminary Injunction (stay) (CCP 527,1094.5)
And Declaratory Relief.

Petitioner Ruth Rodgers' motion to stay a decision and order of the Medical Board of California is denied. As Rodgers states, she was required to show that "(1) the public interest will not suffer if the stay is issued, and (2) the agency is not likely to prevail on the merits. (Code Civ. Proc. 1094.5(h)(1); Medical Board v. Superior Court (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 1458, 1461; Board of Medical Quality Assurance v. Superior Court (1980) 114 Cal.App.3d 272, 275.)" Based on the motion record before me, I cannot make those findings. The substantive declaratory relief also sought by Rodgers' motion may be addressed in an administrative writ proceeding if Rodgers is not satisfied with clarification she seeks from the board. The parties are urged to set the pending writ applications for hearing.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
